content.text: The core seam is clear: lifecycle policy belongs in the pure handler and R2 adapter, while expiration should be represented as object metadata and checked on reads. I’m running the existing unit suite once to confirm the current contract before finalizing the minimal test matrix.

Output:

content.text:
The smallest safe contract is logical expiry on read, stored as R2 custom metadata. New uploads expire after 14 days; expired objects return the existing `404 NOT_FOUND` response but remain physically present. Legacy objects without expiry metadata should remain readable to avoid a breaking rollout.

Evidence and implementation seams:

- [`src/lib/static.ts`](/Users/yusoofsh/Developer/Community/leenk/src/lib/static.ts:7) defines the internal metadata/object/storage contracts.
- [`handleStaticFileRequest()`](/Users/yusoofsh/Developer/Community/leenk/src/lib/static.ts:36) routes public `GET`/`HEAD`, authenticated `POST`, and authenticated `DELETE`.
- [`uploadObject()`](/Users/yusoofsh/Developer/Community/leenk/src/lib/static.ts:82) builds metadata and returns the additive upload JSON at lines 128–139.
- [`serveObject()`](/Users/yusoofsh/Developer/Community/leenk/src/lib/static.ts:186) is the single read-policy seam for both `GET` and `HEAD`; it currently only distinguishes missing from present.
- [`requestMetadata()`](/Users/yusoofsh/Developer/Community/leenk/src/lib/static.ts:219) is the upload metadata constructor.
- [`createStorage()`](/Users/yusoofsh/Developer/Community/leenk/src/pages/static/[...path].ts:33) is the only R2 adapter. `put()` currently writes only `httpMetadata` at line 47; `toStaticFileObject()` reads only HTTP metadata at lines 11–30. Installed R2 types support `customMetadata` on objects and puts.
- Physical deletion is isolated in [`deleteObject()`](/Users/yusoofsh/Developer/Community/leenk/src/lib/static.ts:142) and the adapter’s `bucket.delete()` at [`[...path].ts:35`](/Users/yusoofsh/Developer/Community/leenk/src/pages/static/[...path].ts:35). Expiry must never call either.
- Current tests use an in-memory storage seam at [`src/lib/static.test.ts:11`](/Users/yusoofsh/Developer/Community/leenk/src/lib/static.test.ts:11), with upload assertions at line 99, GET at 139, HEAD at 171, missing-object behavior at 197, and physical-delete coverage at 274–411.
- Static hosting docs are at [`README.md:62`](/Users/yusoofsh/Developer/Community/leenk/README.md:62).
- CI performs frozen install, full verification, and audit at [`.github/workflows/ci.yml:26`](/Users/yusoofsh/Developer/Community/leenk/.github/workflows/ci.yml:26). There is no automatic deployment workflow.
- [`package.json:5`](/Users/yusoofsh/Developer/Community/leenk/package.json:5) defines explicit `wrangler deploy`; `verify` at line 20 runs lint, formatting, types, tests, binding types, and build.
- [`wrangler.jsonc:9`](/Users/yusoofsh/Developer/Community/leenk/wrangler.jsonc:9) only binds the R2 bucket; no lifecycle rule should be added because R2 lifecycle expiration would physically delete objects.

Recommended additive contract:

- Add optional `expiresAt?: Date` to `StaticFileMetadata` or `StaticFileObject`.
- For every new successful `POST`, compute `expiresAt = now + 1_209_600_000 ms`.
- Persist `expiresAt.toISOString()` as R2 `customMetadata.expiresAt`; keep HTTP metadata unchanged.
- Add `expiresAt` as an ISO-8601 string to the existing `201` JSON. Existing consumers remain compatible.
- Return an HTTP `Expires` header on successful `GET` and `HEAD`.
- Before constructing a read response, treat `expiresAt <= now` exactly like absence: existing `404` body `{ error: { code: "NOT_FOUND", message: "Static file not found" } }`, with `Cache-Control: no-store`.
- Do not expose whether an object is missing or expired.
- Missing expiry metadata means a legacy object and remains readable. Malformed present metadata should throw and produce the existing `500`, avoiding accidental indefinite exposure.
- No new upload header is needed for the first slice; “default 14 days” can be the full initial policy. An optional override can be added later without changing this response contract.

Minimal tests:

- Upload stores an expiry exactly 14 days from a fixed clock and returns `expiresAt`.
- Future-dated object is served by `GET` and `HEAD`, both with `Expires`.
- Expired object returns the same 404 for `GET` and `HEAD`, and remains in `storage.objects`.
- Legacy object without `expiresAt` remains readable.
- Existing authenticated physical `DELETE` behavior stays unchanged.

The external client at `/Users/yusoofsh/.scripts/leenk-static.sh:180` needs no compatibility change: its upload validation only requires `.url`, `.path`, and numeric `.size`, so an additive `expiresAt` field is accepted.

Verification: existing suite passes, 2 files and 16 tests. Git state is `main...origin/main [ahead 2]` with one unrelated user modification, `.node-version` changed from `26.5.0` to `latest`; no files were edited by this review.
